The customs broker Karla Rodriguez is proactively following up to ensure Mendez has the proper ACE Portal setup and ACE Refund Enrollment active, otherwise the approved refund will be rejected. This shows good service from the customs broker in preventing a potential issue.

emails.context_for_ask_nauta: This email shows La Fabril (supplier) taking responsibility for a shipment shortage where 24 boxes of Aceite Coloso 8/64 oz were accidentally left in their warehouse and not included in container PRRU401699-4 bound for Puerto Rico. This demonstrates good supplier relationship management and proactive communication.

incidents.details: Multiple vessel rollovers for PO6809-12 - cargo has been rolled 4 times due to operational issues and shipper delays. Originally on MSC JUSTICE VIII (ETD 01/02), then MSC SILVANA VIII (ETD 09/02), then MSC OLIA (ETD 16/02), then MSC LEIGH (ETD 20/02), now on MSC MELISSA (ETD 27/02)
incidents.impact_description: Significant delay of approximately 26 days from original ETD, affecting delivery schedule to Busan

incidents.details: Shipper could not reach stacking by 06/02, causing cargo to be rolled from MSC SILVANA VIII to MSC OLIA
incidents.impact_description: Caused additional vessel rollover and schedule disruption

positive_signals.details: BC Global Logistics consistently providing proactive notifications for each vessel change with updated ETD and cut-off dates
Customer acknowledging updates and confirming they are monitoring the new itinerary

actions_required.action: Monitor cargo stacking and ensure shipper meets cut-off deadline

emails.summary: Multiple vessel rollovers for PO6809-12 copper shipment to Busan - cargo rolled 4 times due to operational issues and shipper delays, now scheduled on MSC MELISSA with ETD 27/02/2026
emails.context_for_ask_nauta: This shipment for PO6809-12 (copper to Busan) under booking EBKG15256634 has experienced significant delays with 4 vessel rollovers. The cargo was originally scheduled on MSC JUSTICE VIII departing February 1st, but due to itinerary changes and shipper delays (unable to reach stacking), it has been rolled multiple times and is now on MSC MELISSA departing February 27th. BC Global Logistics has been proactive in communicating each change, and the customer has acknowledged the updates. The delay is approximately 26 days from the original schedule.
